rubric_coverage.evidence: Web prototype uploaded (prototype.zip), but no architecture diagrams or design documents provided.
rubric_coverage.feedback: Upload system architecture diagrams, design rationale, and tech stack documentation to substantiate the prototype.

rubric_coverage.evidence: prototype.zip likely contains source code, but no code quality reports, repo link, or structure overview is available for review.
rubric_coverage.feedback: Provide a link to the Git repository and include linting/test reports to demonstrate code quality and modularity.

rubric_coverage.evidence: No artefacts or deliverables explicitly demonstrate AI integration.
rubric_coverage.feedback: Include API configurations, model training scripts, prompt engineering logs, or other evidence of AI implementation.

rubric_coverage.evidence: No README, API documentation, or user manuals have been uploaded.
rubric_coverage.feedback: Create comprehensive documentation including setup instructions, API references, and user guides.

rubric_coverage.evidence: No slide decks, pitch videos, or presentation materials submitted.
rubric_coverage.feedback: Prepare and upload a presentation deck or recorded demo video summarizing the project.

missing_artefacts: Source code repository link or extracted code structure
AI integration evidence (scripts, API configs, model details)
Project documentation (README, setup guide, API docs)
last_minute_risks: Submitting only the prototype.zip will result in an automatic zero for 45% of the rubric (Documentation and Presentation).
Lack of explicit AI integration evidence risks a zero for the AI Integration Depth criterion (20% of the total grade).
Evaluators cannot assess Code Quality & Modularity reliably from a zipped prototype alone without repo context or reports.
